_meta.description: Phase 4 v8 testnet deploy artifacts (plan-013 U1 + D-XXX-mesh-seg-republish). FRESH republish (not a compatible upgrade): adds `part_labels: vector<String>` to the Model3D struct (and to the ModelPublished event payload) to support per-part semantic tagging of segmented base GLBs (Tripo mesh_segmentation output). Also extends `new_model` and the `publish` entry fn signatures with a `part_labels` parameter, threaded through `validate_publish_inputs` with new bounds (MAX_PARTS=64, MAX_TAG_LEN per existing tag policy) and abort codes (ETooManyParts=39, EPartLabelTooLong=40). WHY fresh republish, not `sui client upgrade`: adding a field to an existing `key` struct mutates on-chain layout — incompatible per Sui upgrade rules. The publish entry fn signature change is independently breaking. Consistent with the v3–v7 republish precedent. v8 re-bootstrapped a fresh TransferPolicy<NftToken> (royalty rule only, D-036 carry-forward). Everything else carries over from v7: license-policy enforcement (D-040); Model3D shared object w/ glb_blob_id (D-037); mint yields a plain owned token; listing is a separate opt-in Kiosk PTB. Per D-009: testnet is the 6/21 submission target; mainnet ceremony deferred to the 8/27 prize window.
_meta.package_version: v8 (Phase 4, plan-013 U1 — Model3D.part_labels + publish-fn signature extension; fresh republish, single package id)
_meta.transfer_policy_rules: royalty_rule ONLY (D-036 carry-forward). Verified at the v8 bootstrap: the bootstrap tx created exactly one RuleKey dynamic field (id 0x540cad40…) keyed by the royalty rule TypeName under kiosk_apps_package_id 0xe308bb3e….
_meta.kiosk_apps_package_id_discovery: Unchanged from v2–v7 (deps pinned identically — apps@7a07937). The deployed TransferPolicy<NftToken> royalty rule TypeName lives under 0xe308bb3e…. Frontend MUST use the address pinned here, NOT the @mysten/kiosk SDK defaults — otherwise confirm_request fails the rule membership check.
